title: Regular expressions should be syntactically valid
description: Ensures that regular expressions are well-formed and avoid common syntax errors.
recommendation: Use a valid regular expression syntax and test it before use.

noncompliant.code:
import re
pattern = re.compile('[0-9]+')
# sys.exit('Noncompliant example')
noncompliant.description: Missing raw string prefix (r) for the regular expression.

compliant.description: Correct usage of regular expressions with raw string prefix.

why_is_this_an_issue: Invalid regular expressions can lead to unexpected behavior, reduced code readability, and potential performance issues.
how_to_fix: Ensure regular expressions are well-formed and use the raw string prefix (r) when needed.

metadata.title: Regular expressions should be syntactically valid
